login.setupRequired: Setup required. Please set a password in your config file or use the NVR_PASSWORD environment variable.
settings.streamingDesc: Configure live streaming protocols and default behavior.
settings.streaming.defaultProtocolHint: Used when opening live view for the first time.
settings.streaming.flvDesc: Low-latency HTTP streaming for browser playback.
settings.streaming.hls.llHlsHint: Low-latency HLS mode (1-2s). Requires compatible player.
settings.streaming.rtmpDesc: Receive live streams from external encoders (OBS, FFmpeg).
settings.streaming.srtDesc: Receive streams over unreliable networks (4G, public internet).
settings.streaming.resourceEstimateDesc: Approximate resource usage per concurrent stream on Raspberry Pi 3B.
settings.streaming.autoStopNoViewHint: Stop pulling stream after no viewers for this duration.
settings.protocolDocsDesc: Learn about each streaming protocol and its trade-offs.
settings.protocolDocs.webrtc.title: WebRTC
settings.protocolDocs.webrtc.desc: Sub-second latency via WHEP (WebRTC-HTTP Egress Protocol). Best for real-time monitoring and PTZ control. Supports both H.264 and H.265 (HEVC) with hardware-accelerated decoding.
settings.protocolDocs.webrtc.pros: Lowest latency, adaptive bitrate, H.264 + H.265 support
settings.protocolDocs.webrtc.cons: High CPU per viewer, limited concurrent streams
settings.protocolDocs.fmp4.title: HTTP fMP4
settings.protocolDocs.fmp4.desc: Fragmented MP4 over HTTP using Media Source Extensions (MSE). Low latency with wide browser support. No WebCodecs API required — works in all modern browsers.
settings.protocolDocs.fmp4.pros: Low latency (~200ms), H.264 + H.265 support, no WebCodecs required, works in all modern browsers
settings.protocolDocs.fmp4.cons: Slightly higher latency than WebCodecs, requires MSE support
settings.protocolDocs.flv.title: HTTP-FLV
settings.protocolDocs.flv.desc: HTTP-based streaming with FLV container. Good balance of latency and compatibility. Works in most browsers with flv.js.
settings.protocolDocs.flv.pros: Low latency (1-3s), simple HTTP delivery, H.265 support
settings.protocolDocs.flv.cons: Requires flv.js library, moderate CPU usage
settings.protocolDocs.hls.title: HLS
settings.protocolDocs.hls.desc: Industry standard HTTP streaming. Highest compatibility — works everywhere. Higher latency but rock-solid reliability.
settings.protocolDocs.hls.pros: Universal compatibility, CDN-friendly, low server CPU
settings.protocolDocs.llHls.title: LL-HLS (Low-Latency HLS)
settings.protocolDocs.llHls.desc: Enhanced HLS with partial segments for reduced latency. Best of both worlds when supported.
settings.protocolDocs.llHls.cons: Requires player support, slightly more complex
live.h265.fallbackMessage: WebRTC does not support H.265 (HEVC) in browsers. Switched to {protocol} for compatibility.
live.wasm.fallbackToHls: WebCodecs 不可用，正在切换至 HLS
live.h265.webrtcUnavailable: H.265 摄像头不支持 WebRTC

settings.hls.title: HLS Configuration
settings.hls.description: TS-HLS (lal) and LL-HLS (lalmax) segment and playback settings. Disabling stops background HLS slicing immediately without restarting lalmax, so active recording pulls are not interrupted. With on-demand slicing enabled, muxers start only when viewers request m3u8/ts and stop after the idle timeout.
settings.hls.onDemandHint: When enabled, slicing starts only while HLS is being watched; when disabled, slicing begins as soon as the pull stream is active
settings.hls.idleTimeoutHint: Stop slicing and clean up temp segments after no HLS requests; recording pulls are unaffected
settings.hls.segmentCountHint: Number of segments in the m3u8 playlist
settings.hls.lalFragmentDurationHint: Target duration for each TS fragment
settings.hls.lalmaxPartDurationHint: LL-HLS partial segment duration, lower = less latency
